ucs.title: [LLM] Poseidon Group IGT-style net.exe reconnaissance burst (T1007/T1049/T1057/T1087)
ucs.description: Hunts the rapid-fire net.exe / net1.exe enumeration spray that the Poseidon Group's custom Information Gathering Tool (IGT) executes after initial foothold to map domain admins, local admins, services, sessions and shares — the signature 'recon burst' described in the Kaspersky Poseidon Group report (G0033).
ucs.splunk: | tstats `summariesonly` count as exec_count, values(Processes.process) as commands, min(_time) as first_seen, max(_time) as last_seen from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name=net.exe OR Processes.process_name=net1.exe) AND NOT (Processes.user IN ("SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") OR Processes.user="*$") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name _time span=5m | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval cmd_blob=lower(mvjoin(commands,"||")) | eval has_user=if(match(cmd_blob,"\bnet1?\.exe\s+user"),1,0) | eval has_group=if(match(cmd_blob,"\bnet1?\.exe\s+group"),1,0) | eval has_localgroup=if(match(cmd_blob,"\bnet1?\.exe\s+localgroup"),1,0) | eval has_view=if(match(cmd_blob,"\bnet1?\.exe\s+view"),1,0) | eval has_session=if(match(cmd_blob,"\bnet1?\.exe\s+session"),1,0) | eval has_share=if(match(cmd_blob,"\bnet1?\.exe\s+share"),1,0) | eval has_accounts=if(match(cmd_blob,"\bnet1?\.exe\s+accounts"),1,0) | eval has_priv_target=if(match(cmd_blob,"domain admins|enterprise admins|administrators"),1,0) | eval distinct_subcmds=has_user+has_group+has_localgroup+has_view+has_session+has_share+has_accounts | where distinct_subcmds>=4 AND (last_seen-first_seen)<300 | sort - last_seen
ucs.kql:
// Poseidon Group IGT recon burst — chained net.exe enumeration in <5min window
let WindowMin = 5m;
let MinDistinctSubcommands = 4;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"           // exclude machine accounts
| where AccountName !in~ ("system","local service","network service")
| where FileName in~ ("net.exe","net1.exe")
| extend SubCmd = tolower(extract(@"(?i)\bnet1?\.exe\s+(\w+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine))
| where SubCmd in ("user","group","localgroup","view","session","share","accounts","start")
   or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("domain admins","enterprise admins","administrators")
| summarize
    ExecCount         = count(),
    DistinctSubCmds   = dcount(SubCmd),
    SubCmds           = make_set(SubCmd),
    SampleCmds        = make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 10),
    Parent            = any(InitiatingProcessFileName),
    ParentCmd         = any(InitiatingProcessCommandLine),
    HitsPrivKeywords  = countif(ProcessCommandLine has_any ("domain admins","enterprise admins","administrators")),
    FirstSeen         = min(Timestamp),
    LastSeen          = max(Timestamp)
    by DeviceName, AccountName, bin(Timestamp, WindowMin)
| where DistinctSubCmds >= MinDistinctSubcommands       // IGT touches user+group+view+session+...
| where datetime_diff('second', LastSeen, FirstSeen) < 300
| project FirstSeen, LastSeen, DeviceName, AccountName, ExecCount, DistinctSubCmds, SubCmds, HitsPrivKeywords, Parent, ParentCmd, SampleCmds
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Poseidon Group IGT recon burst — Sentinel via 4688 process create
let WindowMin = 5m;
let MinDistinctSubcommands = 4;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688                                  // process-create (requires CL audit)
| where AccountType == "User"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where NewProcessName endswith "\\net.exe" or NewProcessName endswith "\\net1.exe"
| extend SubCmd = tolower(extract(@"(?i)\bnet1?\.exe\s+(\w+)", 1, CommandLine))
| where SubCmd in ("user","group","localgroup","view","session","share","accounts","start")
   or CommandLine has_any ("domain admins","enterprise admins","administrators")
| summarize
    ExecCount         = count(),
    DistinctSubCmds   = dcount(SubCmd),
    SubCmds           = make_set(SubCmd),
    SampleCmds        = make_set(CommandLine, 10),
    Parent            = any(ParentProcessName),
    HitsPrivKeywords  = countif(CommandLine has_any ("domain admins","enterprise admins","administrators")),
    FirstSeen         = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen          = max(TimeGenerated)
    by Computer, Account, bin(TimeGenerated, WindowMin)
| where DistinctSubCmds >= MinDistinctSubcommands
| where datetime_diff('second', LastSeen, FirstSeen) < 300
| project FirstSeen, LastSeen, Computer, Account, ExecCount, DistinctSubCmds, SubCmds, HitsPrivKeywords, Parent, SampleCmds
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.rationale: Poseidon Group's IGT (Information Gathering Tool) is documented (Kaspersky 2016) as scripting net.exe to enumerate users, groups, sessions, shares, services and especially privileged groups in a short burst right after initial access — this is the actor's most repeatable behaviour across all 35+ documented victims. Helpdesk admins typically run 1-2 net commands ad-hoc; 4+ distinct subcommands in <

ucs.title: [LLM] Poseidon Group masqueraded service binary spawning shell (T1036.005 + T1059.001)
ucs.description: Hunts Poseidon's habit of dropping implants under the file names of legitimate Windows service binaries (svchost.exe, lsass.exe, services.exe, etc.) but in non-system folders, then using them to spawn PowerShell/cmd/net for follow-on activity — this name-collision-in-wrong-path pattern was a recurring artefact across the Poseidon Group toolset (G0033) per the Kaspersky writeup.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `summariesonly` count, values(Processes.process) as child_cmd, values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd, min(_time) as first_seen, max(_time) as last_seen from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wmic.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","rundll32.exe") AND Processes.parent_process_name IN ("svchost.exe","lsass.exe","services.exe","winlogon.exe","csrss.exe","spoolsv.exe","wininit.exe","smss.exe","taskhostw.exe") AND NOT (Processes.parent_process_path LIKE "C:\\Windows\\System32\\%" OR Processes.parent_process_path LIKE "C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\%" OR Processes.parent_process_path LIKE "C:\\Windows\\WinSxS\\%" OR Processes.parent_process_path LIKE "C:\\Windows\\servicing\\%") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | sort - last_seen
ucs.kql:
// Poseidon Group — system-binary name in non-system path spawning shell/lolbin
let SystemBinNames = dynamic(["svchost.exe","lsass.exe","services.exe","winlogon.exe","csrss.exe","spoolsv.exe","wininit.exe","smss.exe","taskhostw.exe"]);
let ShellChildren  = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wmic.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe"]);
let LegitParentRoots = dynamic([@"c:\windows\system32\", @"c:\windows\syswow64\", @"c:\windows\winsxs\", @"c:\windows\servicing\"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (SystemBinNames)
| extend ParentPathLower = tolower(InitiatingProcessFolderPath)
| where not (ParentPathLower has_any (LegitParentRoots))    // masqueraded — name matches but path doesn't
| where FileName in~ (ShellChildren)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          MasqueradedParentName = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          MasqueradedParentPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentSHA256          = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          ParentCmd             = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildName             = FileName,
          ChildCmd              = ProcessCommandLine,
          IsRemoteSession       = tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession)
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Poseidon Group — masqueraded system-binary spawning shell (Sentinel 4688)
let SystemBinNames   = dynamic(["svchost.exe","lsass.exe","services.exe","winlogon.exe","csrss.exe","spoolsv.exe","wininit.exe","smss.exe","taskhostw.exe"]);
let ShellChildren    = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wmic.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe"]);
let LegitParentRoots = dynamic([@"c:\windows\system32\", @"c:\windows\syswow64\", @"c:\windows\winsxs\", @"c:\windows\servicing\"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ParentLower = tolower(ParentProcessName), ChildLower = tolower(NewProcessName)
| extend ParentBase = tostring(split(ParentLower, "\\")[-1])
| extend ChildBase  = tostring(split(ChildLower,  "\\")[-1])
| where ParentBase in (SystemBinNames)
| where not (ParentLower has_any (LegitParentRoots))
| where ChildBase in (ShellChildren)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          MasqueradedParentPath = ParentProcessName,
          MasqueradedParentName = ParentBase,
          ChildName             = ChildBase,
          ChildCmd              = CommandLine,
          TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Poseidon Group - System Binary Masquerading Spawning Shell
id: 7a3e2c1b-9f4d-4a8e-b6c3-1d2e5f8a9c0b
status: experimental
description: Detects a process named like a critical Windows system binary executing from outside standard system folders and spawning a script interpreter or LOLBin. Aligns with Poseidon Group (G0033) tradecraft of dropping implants under svchost/lsass/services names in user/temp paths.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0033/
  - https://securelist.com/poseidon-group-a-targeted-attack-boutique/
author: detection-engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.g0033
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent_name:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\svchost.exe'
      - '\lsass.exe'
      - '\services.exe'
      - '\winlogon.exe'
      - '\csrss.exe'
      - '\spoolsv.exe'
      - '\wininit.exe'
      - '\smss.exe'
      - '\taskhostw.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
  filter_legit_parent_path:
    ParentImage|startswith:
      - 'C:\Windows\System32\'
      - 'C:\Windows\SysWOW64\'
      - 'C:\Windows\WinSxS\'
      - 'C:\Windows\servicing\'
  filter_machine_account:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_parent_name and selection_child and not filter_legit_parent_path and not filter_machine_account
falsepositives:
  - Third-party software shipping helper binaries with reused system names (rare; whitelist by ParentImage path + signer)
level: high
ucs.rationale: T1036.005 alone is a generic technique, but Poseidon Group is one of the few documented APTs that consistently named its dropped binaries after core Windows service hosts (svchost/lsass/services) and stored them under user-writable paths — a habit Kaspersky highlighted as a fingerprint of the toolset. Combining the masqueraded-name + non-system-path + shell-child triad eliminates virtually all ben
